stories.title: Experts locate bodies of four missing Italian divers inside Maldives cave

Al Carns, the minister for the armed forces, is being touted by supporters who say the party now needs a radical change if it is to win back thousands of voters who dumped Labour for Nigel Farage’s Reform UK or the left-wing Greens at recent local elections. stories.summary: The former German chancellor added that diplomacy alongside military deterrence is crucial when dealing with Russia. stories.title: EU looks to cow manure to keep food prices down
stories.summary: Brussels’ answer to a looming fertilizer crisis is to make more use of cow dung. Grocery price spikes are on the horizon amid the unending war in Iran and the rising cost of fertilizer. Yet the European Commission’s plan to shore up Europe’s supply, due out Tuesday, centers around a long-term regulatory push to recycle more manure and farm waste into fertilizer.  It’s not the quick fix some were hoping for. Farmers “expected bold action,” said MEP Veronika Vrecionová,

stories.title: Aid cuts and war complicate Ebola response in Congo
stories.summary: Deep foreign aid cuts allowed the deadly Ebola virus to spread undetected in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, while ongoing conflict has complicated efforts to build resilient health care services in the region, experts warned Monday. stories.title: Nancy Pelosi breaks silence to endorse San Francisco supervisor as successor

stories.title: Could the UK really rejoin the EU? - The Latest
stories.summary: The Brexit debate has been reignited after Labour leadership contender Wes Streeting called it a ‘catastrophic mistake’ and said the UK should rejoin the European Union. His comments put pressure on rival Andy Burnham, who has previously advocated for rejoining the bloc but is fighting a byelection in the leave-voting Makerfield constituency. stories.summary: Temperatures in France and England could rise by 15C, while hot air could to give way to snow in parts of US After a prolonged spell of cool conditions across much of Europe, a dramatic swing in temperatures is expected in the coming days as warmer air surges north into western and central parts of the continent. stories.title: Spain has banned Francoist symbols. So why are there still kitsch cafes glorifying the dictator? | Abbas Asaria
stories.summary: These unsettling and unusual places tell a vivid story about the unique way that Spain deals with its past – or fails to Una Grande Libre reads the sign above the entrance to a bar-restaurant in Madrid’s Usera neighbourhood. This was Francisco Franco’s motto for Spain – one, great, free – and it is accompanied by a large portrait of the dictator superimposed on to the window. stories.title: World Urban Forum opens in Baku with warning on global housing divide
stories.summary: Ministers and experts opened the World Urban Forum in Baku on Sunday with stark warnings about the global housing divide, calling for international cooperation to tackle a growing crisis.

stories.title: Iran has responded to latest US peace proposal, foreign ministry official says
stories.summary: On Sunday, Iran's Fars news agency said Washington had presented a five-point list, which included a demand for Iran to keep only one nuclear site in operation and transfer its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to the US.

stories.title: Taiwan’s president defends US arms purchases that Trump called 'bargaining chip'
stories.summary: Trump’s calling of arms sales to Taiwan a “bargaining chip” has raised concerns on the self-governing island of a potential change in US foreign policy towards the territory after the US president’s latest visit to China.

stories.title: North Korea's Kim calls on military to bolster frontline defences against 'arch enemy'
stories.summary: The plans come at a low point in relations with South Korea and Pyongyang unresponsive to Seoul's repeated offers for dialogue.

stories.title: Japan arrests US nationals after one entered enclosure at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo
stories.summary: The baby monkey became an internet star this year after the zoo posted photos of Punch clutching an IKEA plush orangutan for comfort after being rejected by his mother.

stories.title: Bodybuilders show off muscles at Southeast Asian championships
stories.summary: Myanmar hosts the Southeast Asian Bodybuilding Championships in Yangon, with athletes from eight countries competing from May 14 to 18.

stories.title: Arrest of Iraqi terror suspect with alleged links to Iran’s Quds Force is astonishing but not surprising
stories.summary: Iran has a long history of unconventional operations, all designed to divert, distract and destabilise current or potential enemies The arrest by US authorities of an alleged Iraqi commander of an Iranian-backed militia group now accused of responsibility for 18 terrorist attacks in the UK, Europe and Canada since the beginning of the Iran war is an astonishing development – yet not the least bit surprising. According to a complaint unsealed on Friday in a federal court in Manhattan, Mohammad Ba

stories.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
stories.summary: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Vene

stories.title: Putin to visit China days after Trump's Beijing trip, Kremlin says
stories.summary: Putin and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss ways to deepen their countries' "comprehensive partnership and strategic cooperation," the Kremlin said.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.

stories.title: Eurovision final in Vienna overshadowed by protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Vienna against Israel’s participation in Eurovision 2026 as security was tightened around the Wiener Stadthalle before the grand final, amid tensions linked to the war in Gaza.

stories.title: Ebola outbreak is a global health emergency, WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said that it could be a "much larger" outbreak than what is currently being identified and reported but that is does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency.

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports. stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Russian drone attacks on Kyiv, Israeli strikes in Lebanon, Trump in Beijing and a mural of Lamine Yamal – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel confirms Hamas armed wing chief killed in Gaza strike
stories.summary: The military said Haddad was “among the last senior commanders in Hamas’s military wing responsible for planning and carrying out the October 7th massacre.”

stories.title: Israel and Lebanon extend their ceasefire by 45 days following talks in the US
stories.summary: The US State Department will hold negotiations aimed at reaching a permanent political agreement on the 2 and 3 June. Beforehand, the Pentagon is to bring together military delegations from the countries concerned on 29 May.

stories.title: A call to stop the global housing crisis: World Urban Forum, why it matters?
stories.summary: More than 40,000 participants are set to gather in Baku for the UN's World Urban Forum as UN-Habitat warns that 3 billion people lack adequate housing and slum populations could triple by 2050.

stories.title: Trump says 'most active terrorist in the world' killed in US-Nigerian operation
stories.summary: The "meticulously planned and very complex mission" eliminated Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the Islamic State group's second in command globally, Trump said.

stories.title: Trump's independence warning prompts response from Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry said in a statement following Trump's comments that "Beijing has no right to claim jurisdiction over Taiwan."

stories.title: Tensions high in West Bengal after BJP aide shot dead and hundreds arrested
stories.summary: Turmoil and violence rocks state after prime minister Narendra Modi’s party claimed victory in legislative election Tensions have been high in the Indian state of West Bengal after a top political aide from Narendra Modi’s party was shot dead in the street and hundreds were arrested as violence broke out following elections this week. The prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) claimed victory in the West Bengal elections on Monday , defeating Trinamool Congress (TMC), which had ruled over

stories.title: Trump ally Roger Stone condemned for providing lobbying services to Myanmar’s military junta
stories.summary: Stone being paid $50,000 a month to ‘rebuild’ relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government The US lobbyist Roger Stone, a longtime friend and ally of Donald Trump, has been condemned for accepting $50,000 a month to “rebuild” relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Narendra Modi’s BJP wins election in West Bengal for the first time
stories.summary: Result in key Indian state is set to have significant implications for the country’s political landscape Narendra Modi’s party has won a resounding election victory in West Bengal, a state which had been a rare opposition stronghold, expanding his unrivalled consolidation of power across the country. It is the first time that the Indian prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) has won assembly elections in West Bengal, a large and politically significant state in eastern India. Continue rea

stories.title: ‘Defeated by conspiracy’: West Bengal chief minister refuses to resign after election loss
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee says Narendra Modi’s party ‘forcefully captured’ election that ended her party’s 15 years in power A political showdown is taking place in the Indian state of West Bengal as the chief minister, Mamata Banerjee, India’s most powerful female politician, has refused to resign after she lost elections to the prime minister’s party this week. Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) won an overwhelming victory on Monday in state elections in West Bengal, where Banerjee and her Tri

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
